summary: A brief podcast description introducing Mary Midgley's view that philosophy must be a public endeavor. It highlights her belief that philosophical issues should originate from real-world problems rather than purely academic debates.

principles.statement: Philosophy should be a public undertaking concerned with real-world issues.
principles.rationale: Philosophical issues should have their genesis out in the world rather than being confined within the academy.
principles.source_quote: philosophy should be a public undertaking, concerned with issues that have their genesis out in the world rather than within the academy.

anti_patterns.description: Treating philosophy as an exclusively academic exercise.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It disconnects philosophy from the real world, whereas true philosophical issues have their genesis out in the public sphere.
